ogTitle: Surry Hills vs Sussex Inlet: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Surry Hills and Sussex Inlet.
heading: Surry Hills vs Sussex Inlet — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Surry Hills, New South Wales and Sussex Inlet, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Sussex Inlet has a lower overall climate risk score (37/100) compared to Surry Hills (47/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Surry Hills or Sussex Inlet safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Surry Hills has a flood risk score of 48/100, while Sussex Inlet scores 50/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Surry Hills scores 63/100 for bushfire risk. Sussex Inlet scores 23/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Surry Hills or Sussex Inlet?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Sussex Inlet has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.55% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
